   462→                settings = ["ATTRIBUTES": ["Weak"]]
   463→            }
   464→            let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(file: fileRef, settings: settings)
   465→            buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   466→            pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   467→            buildPhase.files?.append(buildFile)
   468→        }
   469→
   470→        for dependency in linkableDependencies {
   471→            switch dependency {
   472→            case let .framework(path, _, _, _, _, _, _, status, condition):
   473→                try addBuildFile(path, condition: condition, status: status)
   474→            case let .library(path, _, _, _, condition):
   475→                try addBuildFile(path, condition: condition)
   476→            case let .xcframework(path, _, _, status, condition):
   477→                try addBuildFile(path, condition: condition, status: status)
   478→            case let .foreignBuildOutput(path, _, condition):
   479→                try addBuildFile(path, condition: condition)
   480→            case .bundle, .macro, .packageProduct:
   481→                break
   482→            case let .product(dependencyTarget, _, status, condition):
   483→                guard status != .none else { continue }
   484→                guard let fileRef = fileElements.product(target: dependencyTarget) else {
   485→                    throw LinkGeneratorError.missingProduct(name: dependencyTarget)
   486→                }
   487→                let settings: [String: BuildFileSetting]? = status == .optional ? ["ATTRIBUTES": ["Weak"]] : nil
   488→                let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(file: fileRef, settings: settings)
   489→                buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   490→                pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   491→                buildPhase.files?.append(buildFile)
   492→            case let .sdk(sdkPath, sdkStatus, _, condition):
   493→                guard sdkStatus != .none else { continue }
   494→                guard let fileRef = fileElements.sdk(path: sdkPath) else {
   495→                    throw LinkGeneratorError.missingReference(path: sdkPath)
   496→                }
   497→
   498→                let buildFile = createSDKBuildFile(for: fileRef, status: sdkStatus)
   499→                buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   500→                pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   501→                buildPhase.files?.append(buildFile)
   502→            }
   503→        }
   504→    }
   505→
   506→    func generateCopyProductsBuildPhase(
   507→        path: AbsolutePath,
   508→        target: Target,
   509→        graphTraverser: GraphTraversing,
   510→        pbxTarget: PBXTarget,
   511→        pbxproj: PBXProj,
   512→        fileElements: ProjectFileElements
   513→    ) throws {
   514→        let dependencies = graphTraverser.copyProductDependencies(path: path, name: target.name).sorted()
   515→
   516→        // If the current target, which is non-shared (e.g., static lib), depends on other focused targets which
   517→        // include Swift code, we must ensure those are treated as dependencies so that Xcode builds the targets
   518→        // in the correct order. Unfortunately, those deps can be part of other projects which would require
   519→        // cross-project references.
   520→        //
   521→        // Thankfully, there's an easy workaround because we can just create a phony copy phase which depends on
   522→        // the outputs of the deps (i.e., the static libs). The copy phase will effectively say "Copy libX.a from
   523→        // Products Dir into Products Dir" which is a nop. To be on the safe side, we're explicitly marking the
   524→        // copy phase as only running for deployment postprocessing (i.e., "Copy only when installing") and
   525→        // disabling deployment postprocessing (it's enabled by default for release builds).
   526→        //
   527→        // This technique also allows resource bundles that reside in different projects to get built ahead of the
   528→        // "Copy Bundle Resources" phase.
   529→        try generateDependenciesBuildPhase(
   530→            dependencies: dependencies,
   531→            dstSubfolderSpec: .productsDirectory,
   532→            buildPhaseName: "Dependencies",
   533→            target: target,
   534→            pbxTarget: pbxTarget,
   535→            pbxproj: pbxproj,
   536→            fileElements: fileElements
   537→        )
   538→
   539→        // For static framewor/library XCFrameworks, we need Xcode to process it to extract the
   540→        // the relevant product within it based on the architecture and place in
   541→        // the products directory. This allows the current target to see the symbols from the XCFramework.
   542→        //
   543→        // Copying to products is not a nop like it is for regular static targets due to the processing step,
   544→        // applying the same technique results in the following error:
   545→        //
   546→        // ```
   547→        // Multiple commands produce  ...
   548→        // ```
   549→        // A slightly different build phase is needed, where the destination is unique per target (this does
   550→        // lead to some wasted derrived data disk space, but only when archiving to limit impact).
   551→        // As of Xcode 14.2, this seems to achieve the desired effect of getting Xcode to process the XCFramework
   552→        // without explicitly linking it nor producing the multiple commands error.
   553→        try generateStaticXCFrameworksDependenciesBuildPhase(
   554→            dependencies: dependencies,
   555→            pbxTarget: pbxTarget,
   556→            pbxproj: pbxproj,
   557→            target: target,
   558→            fileElements: fileElements
   559→        )
   560→    }
   561→
   562→    private func generateDependenciesBuildPhase(
   563→        dependencies: [GraphDependencyReference],
   564→        dstSubfolderSpec: PBXCopyFilesBuildPhase.SubFolder,
   565→        buildPhaseName: String,
   566→        target: Target,
   567→        pbxTarget: PBXTarget,
   568→        pbxproj: PBXProj,
   569→        fileElements: ProjectFileElements
   570→    ) throws {
   571→        var files: [PBXBuildFile] = []
   572→
   573→        for dependency in dependencies.sorted() {
   574→            switch dependency {
   575→            case let .product(target: dependencyTarget, _, _, condition: condition):
   576→                guard let fileRef = fileElements.product(target: dependencyTarget) else {
   577→                    throw LinkGeneratorError.missingProduct(name: dependencyTarget)
   578→                }
   579→
   580→                let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(file: fileRef)
   581→                buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   582→                pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   583→                files.append(buildFile)
   584→            case let .framework(path: path, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, condition),
   585→                 let .library(path: path, _, _, _, condition):
   586→                guard let fileRef = fileElements.file(path: path) else {
   587→                    throw LinkGeneratorError.missingReference(path: path)
   588→                }
   589→                let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(file: fileRef)
   590→                buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   591→                pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   592→                files.append(buildFile)
   593→            default:
   594→                break
   595→            }
   596→        }
   597→
   598→        // Nothing to link, move on.
   599→        if files.isEmpty {
   600→            return
   601→        }
   602→
   603→        let buildPhase = PBXCopyFilesBuildPhase(
   604→            dstPath: nil,
   605→            dstSubfolderSpec: dstSubfolderSpec,
   606→            name: buildPhaseName,
   607→            buildActionMask: 8,
   608→            files: files,
   609→            runOnlyForDeploymentPostprocessing: true
   610→        )
   611→
   612→        pbxproj.add(object: buildPhase)
   613→        pbxTarget.buildPhases.append(buildPhase)
   614→    }
   615→
   616→    private func generateStaticXCFrameworksDependenciesBuildPhase(
   617→        dependencies: [GraphDependencyReference],
   618→        pbxTarget: PBXTarget,
   619→        pbxproj: PBXProj,
   620→        target: Target,
   621→        fileElements: ProjectFileElements
   622→    ) throws {
   623→        var files: [PBXBuildFile] = []
   624→
   625→        for dependency in dependencies.sorted() {
   626→            switch dependency {
   627→            case let .xcframework(path: path, _, _, _, condition):
   628→                guard let fileRef = fileElements.file(path: path) else {
   629→                    throw LinkGeneratorError.missingReference(path: path)
   630→                }
   631→                let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(file: fileRef)
   632→                buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   633→                pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   634→                files.append(buildFile)
   635→            default:
   636→                break
   637→            }
   638→        }
   639→
   640→        if files.isEmpty {
   641→            return
   642→        }
   643→
   644→        // Need a unique (but stable) destination path per target
   645→        // to avoid "multiple commands produce:" errors in Xcode
   646→        let buildPhase = PBXCopyFilesBuildPhase(
   647→            dstPath: "_StaticXCFrameworkDependencies/\(target.name)",
   648→            dstSubfolderSpec: .productsDirectory,
   649→            name: "Static XCFramework Dependencies",
   650→            buildActionMask: 8,
   651→            files: files,
   652→            runOnlyForDeploymentPostprocessing: true
   653→        )
   654→
   655→        pbxproj.add(object: buildPhase)
   656→        pbxTarget.buildPhases.append(buildPhase)
   657→    }
   658→
   659→    func createSDKBuildFile(for fileReference: PBXFileReference, status: LinkingStatus) -> PBXBuildFile {
   660→        var settings: [String: BuildFileSetting]?
   661→        if status == .optional {
   662→            settings = ["ATTRIBUTES": ["Weak"]]
   663→        }
   664→        return PBXBuildFile(
   665→            file: fileReference,
   666→            settings: settings
   667→        )
   668→    }
   669→}

   671→extension PBXTarget {
   672→    func addSwiftPackageProduct(
   673→        productName: String,
   674→        isPlugin: Bool,
   675→        pbxproj: PBXProj,
   676→        target: Target,
   677→        condition: PlatformCondition?
   678→    ) throws {
   679→        let productDependency = XCSwiftPackageProductDependency(productName: productName, isPlugin: isPlugin)
   680→        pbxproj.add(object: productDependency)
   681→
   682→        if isPlugin {
   683→            let pluginDependency = PBXTargetDependency(product: productDependency)
   684→            pbxproj.add(object: pluginDependency)
   685→
   686→            dependencies.append(pluginDependency)
   687→        } else {
   688→            // Build file
   689→            let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(product: productDependency)
   690→            buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   691→            pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   692→
   693→            packageProductDependencies?.append(productDependency)
   694→
   695→            // Link the product
   696→            guard let frameworksBuildPhase = try frameworksBuildPhase() else {
   697→                throw "No frameworks build phase"
   698→            }
   699→
   700→            frameworksBuildPhase.files?.append(buildFile)
   701→        }
   702→    }
   703→}

476→    public func linkableDependencies(
   477→        path: Path.AbsolutePath,
   478→        name: String,
   479→        shouldExcludeHostAppDependencies: Bool
   480→    ) throws -> Set<GraphDependencyReference> {
   481→        guard let target = target(path: path, name: name) else { return Set() }
   482→
   483→        var references = Set<GraphDependencyReference>()
   484→        let targetGraphDependency = GraphDependency.target(name: name, path: path)
   485→
   486→        // System libraries and frameworks
   487→        if target.target.canLinkStaticProducts() {
   488→            let transitiveSystemLibraries = transitiveStaticDependencies(
   489→                from: targetGraphDependency
   490→            )
   491→            .flatMap { dependency -> [GraphDependencyReference] in
   492→                let dependencies = self.graph.dependencies[dependency, default: []]
   493→                return dependencies.compactMap {
   494→                    dependencyDependency -> GraphDependencyReference? in
   495→                    guard case GraphDependency.sdk = dependencyDependency else { return nil }
   496→                    return dependencyReference(
   497→                        to: dependencyDependency, from: targetGraphDependency
   498→                    )
   499→                }
   500→            }
   501→            references.formUnion(transitiveSystemLibraries)
   502→        }
   503→
   504→        // AppClip dependencies
   505→        if target.target.isAppClip {
   506→            let path = try systemFrameworkMetadataProvider.loadMetadata(
   507→                sdkName: "AppClip.framework",
   508→                status: .required,
   509→                platform: .iOS,
   510→                source: .system
   511→            )
   512→            .path
   513→            references.formUnion([
   514→                GraphDependencyReference.sdk(
   515→                    path: path,
   516→                    status: .required,
   517→                    source: .system,
   518→                    condition: .when([.ios])
   519→                ),
   520→            ])
   521→        }
   522→
   523→        // Direct system libraries and frameworks
   524→        let directSystemLibrariesAndFrameworks = graph.dependencies[
   525→            targetGraphDependency, default: []
   526→        ]
   527→        .compactMap { dependency -> GraphDependencyReference? in
   528→            guard case GraphDependency.sdk = dependency else { return nil }
   529→            return dependencyReference(to: dependency, from: targetGraphDependency)
   530→        }
   531→        references.formUnion(directSystemLibrariesAndFrameworks)
   532→
   533→        let precompiledDynamicLibrariesAndFrameworks = precompiledDynamicLibrariesAndFrameworks(
   534→            path: path,
   535→            name: name
   536→        )
   537→
   538→        let staticXCFrameworksLinkedByDynamicXCFrameworkDependencies = filterDependencies(
   539→            from: Set(precompiledDynamicLibrariesAndFrameworks).filter { $0.xcframeworkDependency != nil },
   540→            test: {
   541→                $0.xcframeworkDependency?.linking == .static &&
   542→                    $0.xcframeworkDependency?.swiftModules.isEmpty == false
   543→            },
   544→            skip: { $0.xcframeworkDependency == nil }
   545→        )
   546→
   547→        let libraryDependenciesLinkedByStaticXCFrameworks =
   548→            staticXCFrameworksLinkedByDynamicXCFrameworkDependencies.flatMap {
   549→                guard let dependencies = dependencies[$0] else { return [GraphDependency]() }
   550→                return dependencies.filter {
   551→                    if case GraphDependency.sdk = $0 { true } else { false }
   552→                }
   553→            }
   554→
   555→        let precompiledLibrariesAndFrameworks =
   556→            (
   557→                precompiledDynamicLibrariesAndFrameworks
   558→                    + staticXCFrameworksLinkedByDynamicXCFrameworkDependencies
   559→                    + libraryDependenciesLinkedByStaticXCFrameworks
   560→            )
   561→            .compactMap { dependencyReference(to: $0, from: targetGraphDependency) }
   562→
   563→        references.formUnion(Set(precompiledLibrariesAndFrameworks))
   564→
   565→        // Static libraries and frameworks / Static libraries' dynamic libraries
   566→        if target.target.canLinkStaticProducts() {
   567→            let transitiveStaticTargetReferences = transitiveStaticDependencies(
   568→                from: targetGraphDependency
   569→            )
   570→
   571→            // Exclude any static products linked in a host application
   572→            // however, for search paths it's fine to keep them included
   573→            let hostApplicationStaticTargets: Set<GraphDependency>
   574→            if target.target.product == .unitTests, shouldExcludeHostAppDependencies,
   575→               let hostApp = unitTestHost(path: path, name: name)
   576→            {
   577→                hostApplicationStaticTargets =
   578→                    transitiveStaticDependencies(
   579→                        from: .target(name: hostApp.target.name, path: hostApp.project.path)
   580→                    )
   581→            } else {
   582→                hostApplicationStaticTargets = Set()
   583→            }
   584→
   585→            let staticDependenciesDynamicLibrariesAndFrameworks =
   586→                transitiveStaticTargetReferences.flatMap { dependency in
   587→                    self.graph.dependencies[dependency, default: []]
   588→                        .lazy
   589→                        .filter(\.isTarget)
   590→                        .filter(isEmbeddableDependencyTarget)
   591→                }
   592→
   593→            let staticDependenciesPrecompiledLibrariesAndFrameworks =
   594→                transitiveStaticTargetReferences.flatMap { dependency in
   595→                    self.graph.dependencies[dependency, default: []]
   596→                        .lazy
   597→                        .filter { $0.isPrecompiled && $0.isLinkable }
   598→                }
   599→
   600→            let allDependencies =
   601→                (
   602→                    transitiveStaticTargetReferences
   603→                        + staticDependenciesDynamicLibrariesAndFrameworks
   604→                        + staticDependenciesPrecompiledLibrariesAndFrameworks
   605→                )
   606→
   607→            references.formUnion(
   608→                allDependencies
   609→                    .compactMap { dependencyReference(to: $0, from: targetGraphDependency) }
   610→            )
   611→            references.subtract(
   612→                hostApplicationStaticTargets
   613→                    .compactMap { dependencyReference(to: $0, from: targetGraphDependency) }
   614→            )
   615→        }
   616→
   617→        // Link dynamic libraries and frameworks
   618→        let dynamicLibrariesAndFrameworks = graph.dependencies[
   619→            .target(name: name, path: path), default: []
   620→        ]
   621→        .filter(or(isDependencyDynamicLibrary, isDependencyFramework))
   622→        .compactMap { dependencyReference(to: $0, from: targetGraphDependency) }
   623→
   624→        references.formUnion(dynamicLibrariesAndFrameworks)
   625→

     4→public enum GraphDependencyReference: Equatable, Comparable, Hashable {
     5→    var condition: PlatformCondition? {
     6→        switch self {
     7→        case let .framework(_, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, condition),
     8→             let .library(_, _, _, _, condition),
     9→             let .xcframework(_, _, _, _, condition),
    10→             let .foreignBuildOutput(_, _, condition),
    11→             let .bundle(_, condition),
    12→             let .product(_, _, _, condition),
    13→             let .sdk(_, _, _, condition),
    14→             let .packageProduct(_, condition):
    15→            return condition
    16→        case .macro:
    17→            return nil
    18→        }
    19→    }
    20→
    21→    case macro(path: AbsolutePath)
    22→    case foreignBuildOutput(
    23→        path: AbsolutePath,
    24→        linking: BinaryLinking,
    25→        condition: PlatformCondition? = nil
    26→    )
    27→    case xcframework(
    28→        path: AbsolutePath,
    29→        expectedSignature: String?,
    30→        infoPlist: XCFrameworkInfoPlist,
    31→        status: LinkingStatus,
    32→        condition: PlatformCondition? = nil
    33→    )
    34→    case library(
    35→        path: AbsolutePath,
    36→        linking: BinaryLinking,
    37→        architectures: [BinaryArchitecture],
    38→        product: Product,
    39→        condition: PlatformCondition? = nil
    40→    )
    41→    case framework(
    42→        path: AbsolutePath,
    43→        binaryPath: AbsolutePath,
    44→        dsymPath: AbsolutePath?,
    45→        bcsymbolmapPaths: [AbsolutePath],
    46→        linking: BinaryLinking,
    47→        architectures: [BinaryArchitecture],
    48→        product: Product,
    49→        status: LinkingStatus,
    50→        condition: PlatformCondition? = nil
    51→    )
    52→    case bundle(path: AbsolutePath, condition: PlatformCondition? = nil)
    53→    case product(
    54→        target: String, productName: String, status: LinkingStatus = .required,
    55→        condition: PlatformCondition? = nil
    56→    )
    57→    case sdk(
    58→        path: AbsolutePath, status: LinkingStatus, source: SDKSource,
    59→        condition: PlatformCondition? = nil
    60→    )
    61→    case packageProduct(product: String, condition: PlatformCondition? = nil)
    62→
    63→    init(_ dependency: GraphDependency, condition: PlatformCondition? = nil) {
    64→        switch dependency {
    65→        case let .framework(
    66→            path, binaryPath, dsymPath, bcsymbolmapPaths, linking, architectures, status
    67→        ):
    68→            self = .framework(
    69→                path: path,
    70→                binaryPath: binaryPath,
    71→                dsymPath: dsymPath,
    72→                bcsymbolmapPaths: bcsymbolmapPaths,
    73→                linking: linking,
    74→                architectures: architectures,
    75→                product: (linking == .static) ? .staticFramework : .framework,
    76→                status: status,
    77→                condition: condition
    78→            )
    79→        case let .library(path, _, linking, architectures, _):
    80→            self = .library(
    81→                path: path,
    82→                linking: linking,
    83→                architectures: architectures,
    84→                product: (linking == .static) ? .staticLibrary : .dynamicLibrary,
    85→                condition: condition
    86→            )
    87→        case let .xcframework(xcframework):
    88→            self = .xcframework(
    89→                path: xcframework.path,
    90→                expectedSignature: xcframework.expectedSignature,
    91→                infoPlist: xcframework.infoPlist,
    92→                status: xcframework.status,
    93→                condition: condition
    94→            )
    95→        case let .foreignBuildOutput(output):
    96→            self = .foreignBuildOutput(
    97→                path: output.path,
    98→                linking: output.linking,
    99→                condition: condition
   100→            )
   101→        default:
   102→            preconditionFailure("unsupported dependencies")
   103→        }
   104→    }

   107→        Synthesized(dependencyReference: self).hash(into: &hasher)
   108→    }

   110→    /// For dependencies that exists in the file system (precompiled frameworks & libraries),
   111→    /// this attribute returns the path to them.
   112→    public var precompiledPath: AbsolutePath? {
   113→        switch self {
   114→        case let .framework(path, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _):
   115→            return path
   116→        case let .library(path, _, _, _, _):
   117→            return path
   118→        case let .xcframework(path, _, _, _, _):
   119→            return path
   120→        case let .foreignBuildOutput(path, _, _):
   121→            return path
   122→        default:
   123→            return nil
   124→        }

   127→    public static func < (lhs: GraphDependencyReference, rhs: GraphDependencyReference) -> Bool {

   131→    /// Private helper type to auto-synthesize the hashable & comparable implementations

   118→    public func hash(into hasher: inout Hasher) {
   119→        switch self {
   120→        case let .macro(path):
   121→            hasher.combine(path)
   122→        case let .foreignBuildOutput(output):
   123→            hasher.combine("foreignBuildOutput")
   124→            hasher.combine(output)
   125→        case let .xcframework(xcframework):
   126→            hasher.combine(xcframework)
   127→        case let .framework(path, _, _, _, _, _, _):
   128→            hasher.combine("framework")
   129→            hasher.combine(path)
   130→        case let .library(path, _, _, _, _):
   131→            hasher.combine("library")
   132→            hasher.combine(path)
   133→        case let .bundle(path):
   134→            hasher.combine("bundle")
   135→            hasher.combine(path)
   136→        case let .packageProduct(path, product, isPlugin):
   137→            hasher.combine("package")
   138→            hasher.combine(path)
   139→            hasher.combine(product)
   140→            hasher.combine(isPlugin)
   141→        case let .target(name, path, _):
   142→            hasher.combine("target")
   143→            hasher.combine(name)
   144→            hasher.combine(path)
   145→        case let .sdk(name, path, status, source):
   146→            hasher.combine("sdk")
   147→            hasher.combine(name)
   148→            hasher.combine(path)
   149→            hasher.combine(status)

   151→        }
   152→    }

+    private func transitiveStaticDependenciesOfDynamicFrameworkDependencies(
+        path: Path.AbsolutePath,
+        name: String
+    ) -> Set<GraphDependencyReference> {
+        let targetGraphDependency = GraphDependency.target(name: name, path: path)
+
+        let directDynamicDeps = graph.dependencies[targetGraphDependency, default: []]
+            .filter(or(isDependencyDynamicLibrary, isDependencyFramework))
+
+        let transitiveStaticDeps = directDynamicDeps.flatMap { dynamicDep in
+            transitiveStaticDependencies(from: dynamicDep)
+        }
+
+        return Set(transitiveStaticDeps.compactMap {
+            dependencyReference(to: $0, from: targetGraphDependency)
+        })
+    }

# FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS Setup in Tuist - Investigation Summary

## Key Findings

### 1. How FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS is Set (LinkGenerator.swift)

**Location:** `/cli/Sources/TuistGenerator/Generator/LinkGenerator.swift:296-322`

```swift
func setupFrameworkSearchPath(
    target: Target,
    pbxTarget: PBXTarget,
    sourceRootPath: AbsolutePath,
    path: AbsolutePath,
    graphTraverser: GraphTraversing
) throws {
    let linkableModules = try graphTraverser.searchablePathDependencies(path: path, name: target.name).sorted()
    
    let precompiledPaths = linkableModules.compactMap(\.precompiledPath)
        .map { LinkGeneratorPath.absolutePath($0.removingLastComponent()) }
    let sdkPaths = linkableModules.compactMap { (dependency: GraphDependencyReference) -> LinkGeneratorPath? in
        if case let GraphDependencyReference.sdk(_, _, source, _) = dependency {
            return source.frameworkSearchPath.map { LinkGeneratorPath.string($0) }
        } else {
            return nil
        }
    }
    
    let uniquePaths = Array(Set(precompiledPaths + sdkPaths))
    try setup(
        setting: "FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS",
        paths: uniquePaths,
        pbxTarget: pbxTarget,
        sourceRootPath: sourceRootPath
    )
}
```

**Key Point:** Only dependencies with a `precompiledPath` get added to FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS.

### 2. searchablePathDependencies Method (GraphTraverser.swift:461-467)

**Location:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphTraverser.swift:461-467`

```swift
public func searchablePathDependencies(path: Path.AbsolutePath, name: String) throws -> Set<
    GraphDependencyReference
> {
    try linkableDependencies(path: path, name: name, shouldExcludeHostAppDependencies: false)
        .union(staticPrecompiledFrameworksDependencies(path: path, name: name))
        .union(transitiveStaticDependenciesOfDynamicFrameworkDependencies(path: path, name: name))
}
```

This method returns three sets of dependencies:

1. **linkableDependencies**: Direct linkable dependencies (frameworks, libraries, targets, SDKs)
2. **staticPrecompiledFrameworksDependencies**: Direct static precompiled frameworks only
3. **transitiveStaticDependenciesOfDynamicFrameworkDependencies**: **NEW IN PR #9681** - Transitive static dependencies through dynamic framework dependencies

### 3. transitiveStaticDependenciesOfDynamicFrameworkDependencies (NEW - PR #9681)

**Location:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphTraverser.swift:1700-1716`

```swift
private func transitiveStaticDependenciesOfDynamicFrameworkDependencies(
    path: Path.AbsolutePath,
    name: String
) -> Set<GraphDependencyReference> {
    let targetGraphDependency = GraphDependency.target(name: name, path: path)

    let directDynamicDeps = graph.dependencies[targetGraphDependency, default: []]
        .filter(or(isDependencyDynamicLibrary, isDependencyFramework))

    let transitiveStaticDeps = directDynamicDeps.flatMap { dynamicDep in
        transitiveStaticDependencies(from: dynamicDep)
    }

    return Set(transitiveStaticDeps.compactMap {
        dependencyReference(to: $0, from: targetGraphDependency)
    })
}
```

**What it does:**
1. Gets direct dynamic framework/library dependencies of the target
2. For each dynamic dependency, traverses to find ALL transitive static dependencies
3. Returns those transitive static deps as GraphDependencyReference (converted via `dependencyReference`)

### 4. GraphDependencyReference.precompiledPath Property

**Location:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphDependencyReference.swift:112-125`

```swift
public var precompiledPath: AbsolutePath? {
    switch self {
    case let .framework(path, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _):
        return path
    case let .library(path, _, _, _, _):
        return path
    case let .xcframework(path, _, _, _, _):
        return path
    case let .foreignBuildOutput(path, _, _):
        return path
    default:
        return nil
    }
}
```

**Critical Issue:** `.packageProduct` case returns `nil`, so SPM packages are NEVER added to FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS!

## THE ROOT PROBLEM

### Package Products Have No precompiledPath

**Location of packageProduct definition:** `/cli/Sources/XcodeGraph/Sources/XcodeGraph/Graph/GraphDependency.swift:110`

```swift
case packageProduct(path: AbsolutePath, product: String, type: PackageProductType)
```

**Location of packageProduct reference type:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphDependencyReference.swift:61`

```swift
case packageProduct(product: String, condition: PlatformCondition? = nil)
```

The `.packageProduct` GraphDependencyReference case has NO path information, and its `precompiledPath` returns `nil`. This means:

1. When a target depends on a SPM package product directly -> NOT added to FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS
2. When a target depends on a dynamic framework that depends on a SPM package -> The SPM package is NOT a precompiled dependency, so it doesn't get picked up by the transitive search path logic

### Graph Dependencies for Packages Are Never Built

**Location:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphLoader.swift:317-330`

```swift
private func loadPackage(
    fromPath path: AbsolutePath,
    productName: String,
    type: GraphDependency.PackageProductType
) throws -> GraphDependency {
    // TODO: `fromPath` isn't quite correct as it reflects the path where the dependency was declared
    // and doesn't uniquely identify it. It's been copied from the previous implementation to maintain
    // existing behaviour and should be fixed separately
    .packageProduct(
        path: fromPath,
        product: productName,
        type: type
    )
}
```

**Critical Issue:** When a packageProduct is loaded, there is NO code that:
1. Parses the package's `Package.swift` file
2. Loads the package's dependencies (which are other SPM packages)
3. Adds those dependencies to `graph.dependencies[packageProduct]`

This means when you have:
- App -> FirebaseFeatureModuleImp (dynamic framework) -> FirebaseCrashlytics (SPM static framework)
- The graph has no knowledge that FirebaseFeatureModuleImp depends on FirebaseCrashlytics
- So FirebaseCrashlytics is NOT included in searchablePathDependencies
- So its framework search path is NOT added
- Result: "framework 'FirebaseCrashlytics' not found"

## The Transitive Search Paths Fix (PR #9681)

This only works when the dependency chain is:
- Target -> Dynamic Framework (buildable target) -> Static Dependency (buildable target)

It does NOT work when:
- Target -> Dynamic Framework -> SPM Package (which is never in graph.dependencies)

The fix addresses transitive dependencies through **dynamic framework targets** (local targets in the project), not through SPM packages.

## What's Missing

For SPM package products to work correctly in searchablePathDependencies:

1. When a `.packageProduct` is loaded in GraphLoader, we need to resolve its dependencies
2. We need to add those dependencies to `graph.dependencies[packageProduct]`
3. Package products need to be able to provide their precompiled path(s) or at least their dependencies need to be traversed

OR

1. Package products need to include their transitive dependencies upfront when loaded
2. Those dependencies need to be added to the target that depends on the package product

Currently, SPM packages are treated as black boxes with no dependency information.

HEREDOC

```swift
private func transitiveStaticDependenciesOfDynamicFrameworkDependencies(
    path: Path.AbsolutePath,
    name: String
) -> Set<GraphDependencyReference> {
    let targetGraphDependency = GraphDependency.target(name: name, path: path)

    let directDynamicDeps = graph.dependencies[targetGraphDependency, default: []]
        .filter(or(isDependencyDynamicLibrary, isDependencyFramework))

    let transitiveStaticDeps = directDynamicDeps.flatMap { dynamicDep in
        transitiveStaticDependencies(from: dynamicDep)
    }

    return Set(transitiveStaticDeps.compactMap {
        dependencyReference(to: $0, from: targetGraphDependency)
    })
}
```

**What it does:**
1. Gets direct dynamic framework/library dependencies of the target
2. For each dynamic dependency, traverses to find ALL transitive static dependencies
3. Returns those transitive static deps as GraphDependencyReference (converted via `dependencyReference`)

### 4. GraphDependencyReference.precompiledPath Property

**Location:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphDependencyReference.swift:112-125`

```swift
public var precompiledPath: AbsolutePath? {
    switch self {
    case let .framework(path, _, _, _, _, _, _, _, _):
        return path
    case let .library(path, _, _, _, _):
        return path
    case let .xcframework(path, _, _, _, _):
        return path
    case let .foreignBuildOutput(path, _, _):
        return path
    default:
        return nil
    }
}
```

**Critical Issue:** `.packageProduct` case returns `nil`, so SPM packages are NEVER added to FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS!

## THE ROOT PROBLEM

### Package Products Have No precompiledPath

**Location of packageProduct definition:** `/cli/Sources/XcodeGraph/Sources/XcodeGraph/Graph/GraphDependency.swift:110`

```swift
case packageProduct(path: AbsolutePath, product: String, type: PackageProductType)
```

**Location of packageProduct reference type:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphDependencyReference.swift:61`

```swift
case packageProduct(product: String, condition: PlatformCondition? = nil)
```

The `.packageProduct` GraphDependencyReference case has NO path information, and its `precompiledPath` returns `nil`. This means:

1. When a target depends on a SPM package product directly -> NOT added to FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS
2. When a target depends on a dynamic framework that depends on a SPM package -> The SPM package is NOT a precompiled dependency, so it doesn't get picked up by the transitive search path logic

### Graph Dependencies for Packages Are Never Built

**Location:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphLoader.swift:317-330`

```swift
private func loadPackage(
    fromPath path: AbsolutePath,
    productName: String,
    type: GraphDependency.PackageProductType
) throws -> GraphDependency {
    // TODO: `fromPath` isn't quite correct as it reflects the path where the dependency was declared
    // and doesn't uniquely identify it. It's been copied from the previous implementation to maintain
    // existing behaviour and should be fixed separately
    .packageProduct(
        path: fromPath,
        product: productName,
        type: type
    )
}
```

**Critical Issue:** When a packageProduct is loaded, there is NO code that:
1. Parses the package's `Package.swift` file
2. Loads the package's dependencies (which are other SPM packages)
3. Adds those dependencies to `graph.dependencies[packageProduct]`

This means when you have:
- App -> FirebaseFeatureModuleImp (dynamic framework) -> FirebaseCrashlytics (SPM static framework)
- The graph has no knowledge that FirebaseFeatureModuleImp depends on FirebaseCrashlytics
- So FirebaseCrashlytics is NOT included in searchablePathDependencies
- So its framework search path is NOT added
- Result: "framework 'FirebaseCrashlytics' not found"

## The Transitive Search Paths Fix (PR #9681)

This only works when the dependency chain is:
- Target -> Dynamic Framework (buildable target) -> Static Dependency (buildable target)

It does NOT work when:
- Target -> Dynamic Framework -> SPM Package (which is never in graph.dependencies)

The fix addresses transitive dependencies through **dynamic framework targets** (local targets in the project), not through SPM packages.

## What's Missing

For SPM package products to work correctly in searchablePathDependencies:

1. When a `.packageProduct` is loaded in GraphLoader, we need to resolve its dependencies
2. We need to add those dependencies to `graph.dependencies[packageProduct]`
3. Package products need to be able to provide their precompiled path(s) or at least their dependencies need to be traversed

OR

1. Package products need to include their transitive dependencies upfront when loaded
2. Those dependencies need to be added to the target that depends on the package product

Currently, SPM packages are treated as black boxes with no dependency information.

## Analysis: How Tuist Sets Up FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS for SPM Package Dependencies

Based on my thorough investigation of the Tuist codebase, I've identified exactly how FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS is configured and **why your fixture project is failing**.

### 1. How FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS is Set

**File:** `/cli/Sources/TuistGenerator/Generator/LinkGenerator.swift:296-322`

The `setupFrameworkSearchPath` function retrieves searchable dependencies and extracts their precompiled paths:

```swift
func setupFrameworkSearchPath(
    target: Target,
    pbxTarget: PBXTarget,
    sourceRootPath: AbsolutePath,
    path: AbsolutePath,
    graphTraverser: GraphTraversing
) throws {
    // Gets all searchable path dependencies (includes transitive statics through dynamic frameworks)
    let linkableModules = try graphTraverser.searchablePathDependencies(path: path, name: target.name).sorted()
    
    // Only dependencies with a precompiledPath are added to FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS
    let precompiledPaths = linkableModules.compactMap(\.precompiledPath)
        .map { LinkGeneratorPath.absolutePath($0.removingLastComponent()) }
    let sdkPaths = linkableModules.compactMap { (dependency: GraphDependencyReference) -> LinkGeneratorPath? in
        if case let GraphDependencyReference.sdk(_, _, source, _) = dependency {
            return source.frameworkSearchPath.map { LinkGeneratorPath.string($0) }
        } else {
            return nil
        }
    }
    
    let uniquePaths = Array(Set(precompiledPaths + sdkPaths))
    try setup(
        setting: "FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS",
        paths: uniquePaths,
        pbxTarget: pbxTarget,
        sourceRootPath: sourceRootPath
    )
}
```

**Key insight:** Only dependencies with a `precompiledPath` value get added to `FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS`.

### 2. The searchablePathDependencies Method

**File:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphTraverser.swift:461-467`

This method assembles three categories of dependencies:

```swift
public func searchablePathDependencies(path: Path.AbsolutePath, name: String) throws -> Set<
    GraphDependencyReference
> {
    try linkableDependencies(path: path, name: name, shouldExcludeHostAppDependencies: false)
        .union(staticPrecompiledFrameworksDependencies(path: path, name: name))
        .union(transitiveStaticDependenciesOfDynamicFrameworkDependencies(path: path, name: name))
}
```

1. **linkableDependencies** - Direct linkable deps (frameworks, libraries, targets, SDKs)
2. **staticPrecompiledFrameworksDependencies** - Direct static precompiled frameworks
3. **transitiveStaticDependenciesOfDynamicFrameworkDependencies** - **NEW in PR #9681** - Transitive statics through dynamic frameworks

### 3. The Transitive Search Paths Fix (PR #9681)

**File:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphTraverser.swift:1700-1716`

```swift

        .filter(or(isDependencyDynamicLibrary, isDependencyFramework))

    // Find transitive statics through each dynamic dep
    let transitiveStaticDeps = directDynamicDeps.flatMap { dynamicDep in
        transitiveStaticDependencies(from: dynamicDep)
    }

    return Set(transitiveStaticDeps.compactMap {
        dependencyReference(to: $0, from: targetGraphDependency)
    })
}
```

It works by:
1. Finding all direct dynamic framework/library dependencies
2. For each dynamic dependency, traversing the graph to find transitive static dependencies
3. Converting those to GraphDependencyReference objects

**The traversal logic** (`filterDependencies` at line 1143):

### 4. GraphDependencyReference.precompiledPath

**File:** `/cli/Sources/TuistCore/Graph/GraphDependencyReference.swift:112-125`

```swift

```

content.text:
Good, so `.tuist()` projects convert SPM packages into native targets in external projects. These targets would appear in the graph as regular targets with `project.type == .external`. So the dependency graph should work correctly.

Let me actually re-download the fixture and verify what's really going on at runtime. The key question is: does the generated project from the fixture have the `ExplicitDependencyGraphMapper` active? We saw it doesn't. But then what's generating the `FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS`?

Let me look at the actual build error from the issue reporter more carefully. The paths in their error show `Build/Products/Debug-iphonesimulator/FirebaseFeatureModuleImp.framework/` -- there's no scoped subdirectory. Without `enforceExplicitDependencies`, frameworks are built into the standard flat `Build/Products/Debug-iphonesimulator/` directory.

The real issue is that when the Swift compiler processes the app's import of `FirebaseFeatureModuleImp`, it loads the `.swiftmodule` and discovers that module depends on `FirebaseCrashlytics`. The compiler then needs to find `FirebaseCrashlytics` module, but the app target doesn't have the right search paths to find it.

Let me check how `FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS` is set for non-explicit-dependency scenarios:

Now I see the key issue. At line 1636-1644, when a dependency is a `.target`, `dependencyReference` returns a `.product` which has **no `precompiledPath`**. And at line 1625-1628, `.packageProduct` of non-runtime type also returns `nil`.

So even though `transitiveStaticDependenciesOfDynamicFrameworkDependencies` correctly traverses the graph and finds the transitive static target dependencies (like CocoaLumberjack, SVGKit, FirebaseCrashlytics - which are all native targets in external projects when resolved via `tuist install`), when they go through `dependencyReference`, they become `.product` references, and `.product` has `precompiledPath == nil`, so they get filtered out in `setupFrameworkSearchPath`.

This is exactly the problem. Let me verify:

content.text:
Confirmed. `.product` (which is what target dependencies become) returns `nil` from `precompiledPath`. So `setupFrameworkSearchPath` in `LinkGenerator` filters them out.

**But this is by design for the non-explicit-dependencies case.** Without `enforceExplicitDependencies`, all targets build into the same flat `BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR`. Xcode automatically resolves frameworks in that directory. There's no need for extra `FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS` because everything is in the default search path.

So the question becomes: why does the reporter's build fail? Let me think about this differently.

The error `Unable to find module dependency: 'FirebaseCrashlytics'` happens during Swift compilation, not linking. The Swift compiler finds `FirebaseFeatureModuleImp.swiftmodule` and sees it imports `FirebaseCrashlytics`. To type-check the app's usage of `FirebaseFeatureModuleImp`, it needs to also load `FirebaseCrashlytics.swiftmodule`. Without explicit dependencies, all modules should be in the same `Build/Products/Debug-iphonesimulator/` directory.

Unless... the external project targets DO get scoped build directories even without `enforceExplicitDependencies`. Let me check:

content.text:
OK, `BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR` is only overridden by `ExplicitDependencyGraphMapper`, nowhere else. So without it, all targets build into the flat directory and the Swift compiler should find everything.

I think the reporter's issue might actually be Xcode version or build ordering related. Or it could be that they have `enforceExplicitDependencies: true` in their actual project but not in the reduced fixture. Let me check the issue comments for more context:

input.command: mise exec gh -- gh issue view 9676 --repo tuist/tuist --json comments --jq '.comments[].body' 2>&1

content.content:
TUI-518

This looks like an issue with generated projects. I'd recommend installing [this debug skill](https://github.com/tuist/tuist/blob/main/skills/skills/debug-generated-project/SKILL.md) following the steps in the [agent-skills repo](https://github.com/tuist/agent-skills) and using a coding agent (like Claude, Codex, or Amp) to debug the issue. That said, based on similar reports, this error pattern often stems from **Tuist requiring explicit declaration of all dependencies**, including transitive ones. A few things to try: 1. **Run `tuist inspect implicit-imports`** — this will identify any transitive dependencies that need to be explicitly added to your targets' dependencies arrays. Firebase packages have complex dependency trees (GoogleUtilities, GoogleAppMeasurement, etc.) that often need explicit declaration. 2. **Check your SPM integration mode** — if you're using [XcodeProj-based SPM integration](https://github.com/tuist/tuist/issues/9286#issuecomment-3817424099), there's a known architectural limitation where external frameworks build into separate DerivedData directories. Consider using Xcode's native SPM integration or ensuring you build the full workspace. 3. **Try `--force-resolved-versions`** — this ensures SPM respects your Package.resolved and doesn't pull unexpected versions. If the debug skill doesn't surface a solution, feel free to share more details from your minimal reproducible sample on the [community forum](https://community.tuist.dev) for a deeper investigation. *To reply, just mention [@dosu](https://go.dosu.dev/dosubot).* --- How did I do? This looks like an issue with generated projects. That said, based on similar reports, this error pattern often stems from **Tuist requiring explicit declaration of all dependencies**, including transitive ones. A few things to try: 1. **Run `tuist inspect implicit-imports`** — this will identify any transitive dependencies that need to be explicitly added to your targets' dependencies arrays. Firebase packages have complex dependency trees (GoogleUtilities, GoogleAppMeasurement, etc.) that often need explicit declaration. 2. **Check your SPM integration mode** — if you're using [XcodeProj-based SPM integration](https://github.com/tuist/tuist/issues/9286#issuecomment-3817424099), there's a known architectural limitation where external frameworks build into separate DerivedData directories. Consider using Xcode's native SPM integration or ensuring you build the full workspace. 3. **Try `--force-resolved-versions`** — this ensures SPM respects your Package.resolved and doesn't pull unexpected versions. content.text:
Now I see the actual problem clearly:

- `ViewStylerHelper` depends on `CocoaLumberjack` and `SVGKit` (both SPM packages resolved as static frameworks)
- `ViewStylerHelper` has **no `FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS`** at all
- `FirebaseFeatureModuleImp` only has paths to xcframework artifacts (binary packages), not to source-built SPM targets like `FirebaseCrashlytics`

The SPM packages resolved by `tuist install` come in two forms:
1. **Binary xcframeworks** (like `FirebaseAnalytics.xcframework`) -- these get `FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS` pointing to the artifact directory
2. **Source-built targets** (like `CocoaLumberjack`, `SVGKit`, `FirebaseCrashlytics`) -- these build into `Build/Products/` as regular frameworks but their paths aren't being added

Without `enforceExplicitDependencies`, everything builds into the same flat directory. But `CocoaLumberjack` and `SVGKit` are **static frameworks** -- they don't produce a `.framework` bundle in the products directory the same way a dynamic framework does. The linker flag `-framework CocoaLumberjack` requires the framework to be in a search path. Since `CocoaLumberjack` is a static framework that gets its `BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR` scoped (because external targets DO get scoped), the linker can't find it.

Wait, we just confirmed there's no `BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR` override. Let me check more carefully:

content.content:
88
Shell cwd was reset to /Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3

content.text:
OK, all 88 occurrences are `sourceTree = BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR` (file references), not build settings. So no target overrides `BUILT_PRODUCTS_DIR`. Everything builds into the same flat directory.

content.text:
The `Dev` scheme doesn't include the external dependency targets. That's the problem! The project-level schemes only reference targets from the main project. The external dependencies in separate xcodeproj files are only in the workspace-level scheme.

When the user builds via the `Dev` scheme (which is in the `.xcodeproj`), Xcode uses implicit dependency resolution. But since the dependencies are in separate xcodeproj files and the scheme is project-scoped, Xcode might not build them or might build them in a way that the modules aren't discoverable.

Actually wait, let me check which scheme the reporter uses. Let me re-try building with the workspace and the Dev scheme:

input.description: Analyze FirebaseCrashlytics target in Firebase project

content.text:
There it is. `MACH_O_TYPE = staticlib` and `DEFINES_MODULE = NO`. `FirebaseCrashlytics` is built as a **static library** (inside a `.framework` wrapper) with **no module definition**. It uses `DEFINES_MODULE = NO` and has its module map defined externally via `-fmodule-map-file`.

The module map for `FirebaseCrashlytics` is referenced via `OTHER_CFLAGS` with `-fmodule-map-file=$(SRCROOT)/../../tuist-derived/FirebaseCrashlytics/FirebaseCrashlytics.modulemap`. This modulemap is only passed to the `FirebaseCrashlytics` target itself (and its direct dependents), but NOT to the **app target**.

The app target imports `FirebaseFeatureModuleImp` (a dynamic framework), which in turn imports `FirebaseCrashlytics`. When the Swift compiler processes the app and loads `FirebaseFeatureModuleImp.swiftmodule`, it sees `FirebaseCrashlytics` as a dependency and tries to find it. But the app doesn't have the `-fmodule-map-file` for `FirebaseCrashlytics`, so it can't resolve the module.

This is a **modulemap propagation** issue, not a `FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS` issue. The fix should be to propagate the `-fmodule-map-file` flags (or equivalent `FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS` / `HEADER_SEARCH_PATHS`) for transitive ObjC dependencies through dynamic frameworks to the app target.

Let me revert our changes and investigate the right fix.

content.text:
Here's what I found:

**The fixture's issue is NOT about `FRAMEWORK_SEARCH_PATHS` or `ExplicitDependencyGraphMapper` at all.** It's a **modulemap propagation** problem.

The root cause:
1. `FirebaseCrashlytics` is an ObjC target compiled as a static library (`MACH_O_TYPE = staticlib`) with `DEFINES_MODULE = NO`
2. Its module is defined via an external modulemap file, passed only to itself and direct dependents via `-fmodule-map-file=.../FirebaseCrashlytics.modulemap` in `OTHER_CFLAGS`/`OTHER_SWIFT_FLAGS`
3. `FirebaseFeatureModuleImp` (a dynamic framework) depends on `FirebaseCrashlytics` and gets that flag, so it compiles fine
4. The **app target** imports `FirebaseFeatureModuleImp`, and the Swift compiler loads its `.swiftmodule`, which declares a dependency on the `FirebaseCrashlytics` module
5. The app target does NOT have the `-fmodule-map-file` for `FirebaseCrashlytics`, so the compiler can't resolve it

Same pattern for `SVGKit`/`CocoaLumberjack`.

**What can be done:**
1. **On Tuist's side:** The `ModuleMapMapper` or `LinkGenerator` (or a new mapper) should propagate `-fmodule-map-file` flags for transitive ObjC/C dependencies through dynamic framework boundaries to consumer targets. When an app depends on a dynamic framework that re-exports an ObjC module (via its swiftmodule), the app needs the modulemap reference too.
2. **On their side (workaround):** They could explicitly add the transitive SPM dependencies to their app target's dependencies, which would cause Tuist to generate the `-fmodule-map-file` flags on the app target. But that defeats the purpose of modularization.

Should I revert the current changes and investigate the modulemap propagation fix instead? That's the actual bug the reporter is hitting.

This causes "Unable to find module dependency" errors.

content.content:
1→import Foundation
     2→import Path
     3→import TuistCore
     4→import TuistLogging
     5→import TuistSupport
     6→import XcodeGraph
     7→
     8→/// Mapper that maps the `MODULE_MAP` build setting to the `-fmodule-map-file` compiler flags.
     9→/// It is required to avoid embedding the module map into the frameworks during cache operations, which would make the framework
    10→/// not portable, as the modulemap could contain absolute paths.
    11→public struct ModuleMapMapper: GraphMapping { // swiftlint:disable:this type_body_length
    12→    private static let modulemapFileSetting = "MODULEMAP_FILE"
    13→    private static let otherCFlagsSetting = "OTHER_CFLAGS"
    14→    private static let otherSwiftFlagsSetting = "OTHER_SWIFT_FLAGS"
    15→    private static let headerSearchPaths = "HEADER_SEARCH_PATHS"
    16→
    17→    private struct TargetID: Hashable {
    18→        let projectPath: AbsolutePath
    19→        let targetName: String
    20→    }
    21→
    22→    private struct DependencyMetadata: Hashable {
    23→        let moduleMapPath: AbsolutePath?
    24→        let headerSearchPaths: [String]
    25→    }
    26→
    27→    public init() {}
    28→
    29→    // swiftlint:disable function_body_length
    30→    public func map(graph: Graph, environment: MapperEnvironment) throws -> (Graph, [SideEffectDescriptor], MapperEnvironment) {
    31→        Logger.current
    32→            .debug(
    33→                "Transforming graph \(graph.name): Mapping MODULE_MAP build setting to -fmodule-map-file compiler flag"
    34→            )
    35→
    36→        var targetToDependenciesMetadata: [TargetID: Set<DependencyMetadata>] = [:]
    37→        let graphTraverser = GraphTraverser(graph: graph)
    38→        for target in graphTraverser.allTargets() {
    39→            try Self.dependenciesModuleMaps(
    40→                graph: graph,
    41→                target: target,
    42→                targetToDependenciesMetadata: &targetToDependenciesMetadata
    43→            )
    44→        }
    45→
    46→        var graph = graph
    47→
    48→        graph.projects = Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: graph.projects.map { projectPath, project in
    49→            var project = project
    50→            project.targets = Dictionary(uniqueKeysWithValues: project.targets.map { targetName, target in
    51→                var target = target
    52→                let targetID = TargetID(projectPath: project.path, targetName: target.name)
    53→                var mappedSettingsDictionary = target.settings?.base ?? [:]
    54→                let hasModuleMap = mappedSettingsDictionary[Self.modulemapFileSetting] != nil
    55→                guard hasModuleMap || !(targetToDependenciesMetadata[targetID]?.isEmpty ?? true)
    56→                else { return (targetName, target) }
    57→
    58→                if hasModuleMap {
    59→                    mappedSettingsDictionary[Self.modulemapFileSetting] = nil
    60→                }
    61→
    62→                if let updatedOtherSwiftFlags = Self.updatedOtherSwiftFlags(
    63→                    targetID: targetID,
    64→                    oldOtherSwiftFlags: mappedSettingsDictionary[Self.otherSwiftFlagsSetting],
    65→                    targetToDependenciesMetadata: targetToDependenciesMetadata
    66→                ) {
    67→                    mappedSettingsDictionary[Self.otherSwiftFlagsSetting] = updatedOtherSwiftFlags
    68→                }
    69→
    70→                if let updatedOtherCFlags = Self.updatedOtherCFlags(
    71→                    targetID: targetID,
    72→                    oldOtherCFlags: mappedSettingsDictionary[Self.otherCFlagsSetting],
    73→                    targetToDependenciesMetadata: targetToDependenciesMetadata
    74→                ) {
    75→                    mappedSettingsDictionary[Self.otherCFlagsSetting] = updatedOtherCFlags
    76→                }
    77→
    78→                if let updatedHeaderSearchPaths = Self.updatedHeaderSearchPaths(
    79→                    targetID: targetID,
    80→                    oldHeaderSearchPaths: mappedSettingsDictionary[Self.headerSearchPaths],
    81→                    targetToDependenciesMetadata: targetToDependenciesMetadata
    82→                ) {
    83→                    mappedSettingsDictionary[Self.headerSearchPaths] = updatedHeaderSearchPaths
    84→                }
    85→
    86→                let targetSettings = target.settings ?? Settings(
    87→                    base: [:],
    88→                    configurations: [:],
    89→                    defaultSettings: project.settings.defaultSettings
    90→                )
    91→                target.settings = targetSettings.with(base: mappedSettingsDictionary)
    92→
    93→                return (target.name, target)

    96→            return (projectPath, project)

   101→    private static func makeProjectsByPathWithTargetsByName(workspace: WorkspaceWithProjects)
   102→        -> ([AbsolutePath: Project], [String: Target])
   103→    {
   104→        var projectsByPath = [AbsolutePath: Project]()
   105→        var targetsByName = [String: Target]()
   106→        for project in workspace.projects {
   107→            projectsByPath[project.path] = project
   108→            for target in project.targets.values {
   109→                targetsByName[target.name] = target
   110→            }
   111→        }
   112→        return (projectsByPath, targetsByName)
   113→    }
   114→
   115→    /// Calculates the set of module maps to be linked to a given target and populates the `targetToDependenciesMetadata`
   116→    /// dictionary.
   117→    /// Each target must link the module map of its direct and indirect dependencies.
   118→    /// The `targetToDependenciesMetadata` is also used as cache to avoid recomputing the set for already computed targets.
   119→    private static func dependenciesModuleMaps( // swiftlint:disable:this function_body_length
   120→        graph: Graph,
   121→        target: GraphTarget,
   122→        targetToDependenciesMetadata: inout [TargetID: Set<DependencyMetadata>]
   123→    ) throws {
   124→        let targetID = TargetID(projectPath: target.path, targetName: target.target.name)
   125→        if targetToDependenciesMetadata[targetID] != nil {
   126→            // already computed
   127→            return
   128→        }
   129→
   130→        let graphTraverser = GraphTraverser(graph: graph)
   131→
   132→        var dependenciesMetadata: Set<DependencyMetadata> = []
   133→        for dependency in graphTraverser.directTargetDependencies(path: target.path, name: target.target.name) {
   134→            try Self.dependenciesModuleMaps(
   135→                graph: graph,
   136→                target: dependency.graphTarget,
   137→                targetToDependenciesMetadata: &targetToDependenciesMetadata
   138→            )
   139→
   140→            // direct dependency module map
   141→            let dependencyModuleMapPath: AbsolutePath?
   142→
   143→            if case let .string(dependencyModuleMap) = dependency.target.settings?.base[Self.modulemapFileSetting] {
   144→                let pathString = dependency.graphTarget.path.pathString
   145→                dependencyModuleMapPath = try AbsolutePath(
   146→                    validating: dependencyModuleMap
   147→                        .replacingOccurrences(of: "$(PROJECT_DIR)", with: pathString)
   148→                        .replacingOccurrences(of: "$(SRCROOT)", with: pathString)
   149→                        .replacingOccurrences(of: "$(SOURCE_ROOT)", with: pathString)
   150→                )
   151→            } else {
   152→                dependencyModuleMapPath = nil
   153→            }
   154→
   155→            var headerSearchPaths: [String]
   156→            switch dependency.target.settings?.base[Self.headerSearchPaths] ?? .array([]) {
   157→            case let .array(values):
   158→                headerSearchPaths = values
   159→            case let .string(value):
   160→                headerSearchPaths = [value]
   161→            }
   162→
   163→            headerSearchPaths = headerSearchPaths.map {
   164→                let pathString = dependency.graphTarget.path.pathString
   165→                return (
   166→                    try? AbsolutePath(
   167→                        validating: $0
   168→                            .replacingOccurrences(of: "$(PROJECT_DIR)", with: pathString)
   169→                            .replacingOccurrences(of: "$(SRCROOT)", with: pathString)
   170→                            .replacingOccurrences(of: "$(SOURCE_ROOT)", with: pathString)
   171→                    ).pathString
   172→                ) ?? $0
   173→            }
   174→
   175→            // indirect dependency module maps
   176→            let dependentTargetID = TargetID(projectPath: dependency.graphTarget.path, targetName: dependency.target.name)
   177→            if let indirectDependencyMetadata = targetToDependenciesMetadata[dependentTargetID] {
   178→                dependenciesMetadata.formUnion(indirectDependencyMetadata)
   179→            }
   180→
   181→            dependenciesMetadata.insert(
   182→                DependencyMetadata(
   183→                    moduleMapPath: dependencyModuleMapPath,
   184→                    headerSearchPaths: headerSearchPaths
   185→                )
   186→            )
   187→        }
   188→
   189→        targetToDependenciesMetadata[targetID] = dependenciesMetadata
   190→    }
   191→
   192→    private static func updatedHeaderSearchPaths(
   193→        targetID: TargetID,
   194→        oldHeaderSearchPaths: SettingsDictionary.Value?,
   195→        targetToDependenciesMetadata: [TargetID: Set<DependencyMetadata>]
   196→    ) -> SettingsDictionary.Value? {
   197→        let dependenciesHeaderSearchPaths = Set(targetToDependenciesMetadata[targetID]?.flatMap(\.headerSearchPaths) ?? [])
   198→        guard !dependenciesHeaderSearchPaths.isEmpty
   199→        else { return nil }
   200→
   201→        var mappedHeaderSearchPaths: [String]
   202→        switch oldHeaderSearchPaths ?? .array(["$(inherited)"]) {
   203→        case let .array(values):
   204→            mappedHeaderSearchPaths = values
   205→        case let .string(value):
   206→            mappedHeaderSearchPaths = value.split(separator: " ").map(String.init)
   207→        }
   208→
   209→        for headerSearchPath in dependenciesHeaderSearchPaths.sorted() {
   210→            mappedHeaderSearchPaths.append(
   211→                (
   212→                    try? AbsolutePath(validating: headerSearchPath)
   213→                        .relative(to: targetID.projectPath).pathString
   214→                ).map { "$(SRCROOT)/\($0)" } ?? headerSearchPath
   215→            )
   216→        }
   217→
   218→        return .array(mappedHeaderSearchPaths)
   219→    }
   220→
   221→    private static func updatedOtherSwiftFlags(
   222→        targetID: TargetID,
   223→        oldOtherSwiftFlags: SettingsDictionary.Value?,
   224→        targetToDependenciesMetadata: [TargetID: Set<DependencyMetadata>]
   225→    ) -> SettingsDictionary.Value? {
   226→        guard let dependenciesModuleMaps = targetToDependenciesMetadata[targetID]?.compactMap(\.moduleMapPath),
   227→              !dependenciesModuleMaps.isEmpty
   228→        else { return nil }
   229→
   230→        var mappedOtherSwiftFlags: [String]
   231→        switch oldOtherSwiftFlags ?? .array(["$(inherited)"]) {
   232→        case let .array(values):
   233→            mappedOtherSwiftFlags = values
   234→        case let .string(value):
   235→            mappedOtherSwiftFlags = value.split(separator: " ").map(String.init)
   236→        }
   237→
   238→        for moduleMap in dependenciesModuleMaps.sorted() {
   239→            mappedOtherSwiftFlags.append(contentsOf: [
   240→                "-Xcc",
   241→                "-fmodule-map-file=$(SRCROOT)/\(moduleMap.relative(to: targetID.projectPath))",
   242→            ])
   243→        }
   244→
   245→        return .array(mappedOtherSwiftFlags)
   246→    }
   247→
   248→    private static func updatedOtherCFlags(
   249→        targetID: TargetID,
   250→        oldOtherCFlags: SettingsDictionary.Value?,
   251→        targetToDependenciesMetadata: [TargetID: Set<DependencyMetadata>]
   252→    ) -> SettingsDictionary.Value? {
   253→        guard let dependenciesModuleMaps = targetToDependenciesMetadata[targetID]?.compactMap(\.moduleMapPath),
   254→              !dependenciesModuleMaps.isEmpty
   255→        else { return nil }
   256→
   257→        var mappedOtherCFlags: [String]
   258→        switch oldOtherCFlags ?? .array(["$(inherited)"]) {
   259→        case let .array(values):
   260→            mappedOtherCFlags = values
   261→        case let .string(value):
   262→            mappedOtherCFlags = value.split(separator: " ").map(String.init)
   263→        }
   264→
   265→        for moduleMap in dependenciesModuleMaps.sorted() {
   266→            mappedOtherCFlags.append("-fmodule-map-file=$(SRCROOT)/\(moduleMap.relative(to: targetID.projectPath))")
   267→        }
   268→
   269→        return .array(mappedOtherCFlags)
   270→    }

    10→enum LinkGeneratorError: FatalError, Equatable {
    11→    case missingProduct(name: String)
    12→    case missingReference(path: AbsolutePath)
    13→    case missingConfigurationList(targetName: String)
    14→
    15→    var description: String {
    16→        switch self {
    17→        case let .missingProduct(name):
    18→            return "Couldn't find a reference for the product \(name)."
    19→        case let .missingReference(path):
    20→            return "Couldn't find a reference for the file at path \(path.pathString)."
    21→        case let .missingConfigurationList(targetName):
    22→            return "The target \(targetName) doesn't have a configuration list."
    23→        }
    24→    }
    25→
    26→    var type: ErrorType {
    27→        switch self {
    28→        case .missingProduct, .missingConfigurationList, .missingReference:
    29→            return .bug
    30→        }
    31→    }
    32→}

    46→/// When generating build settings like "framework search path", some of the path might be relative to paths
    47→/// defined by environment variables like $(DEVELOPER_FRAMEWORKS_DIR). This enum represents both
    48→/// types of supported paths.
    49→enum LinkGeneratorPath: Hashable {
    50→    case absolutePath(AbsolutePath)
    51→    case string(String)
    52→
    53→    func xcodeValue(sourceRootPath: AbsolutePath) -> String {
    54→        switch self {
    55→        case let .absolutePath(path):
    56→            return "$(SRCROOT)/\(path.relative(to: sourceRootPath).pathString)"
    57→        case let .string(value):
    58→            return value
    59→        }
    60→    }
    61→}

   175→    func generatePackages(
   176→        target: Target,
   177→        pbxTarget: PBXTarget,
   178→        pbxproj: PBXProj
   179→    ) throws {
   180→        for dependency in target.dependencies {
   181→            switch dependency {
   182→            case let .package(product: product, type: type, condition: condition):
   183→                try pbxTarget.addSwiftPackageProduct(
   184→                    productName: product,
   185→                    isPlugin: type == .plugin,
   186→                    pbxproj: pbxproj,
   187→                    target: target,
   188→                    condition: condition
   189→                )
   190→            case .framework, .library, .project, .sdk, .target, .xcframework, .xctest:
   191→                break
   192→            }
   193→        }
   194→    }
   195→
   196→    // swiftlint:disable:next function_body_length
   197→    func generateEmbedPhase(
   198→        target: Target,
   199→        pbxTarget: PBXTarget,
   200→        pbxproj: PBXProj,
   201→        fileElements: ProjectFileElements,
   202→        sourceRootPath: AbsolutePath,
   203→        path: AbsolutePath,
   204→        graphTraverser: GraphTraversing
   205→    ) throws {
   206→        let embeddableFrameworks = graphTraverser.embeddableFrameworks(path: path, name: target.name).sorted()
   207→
   208→        let embedPhase = PBXCopyFilesBuildPhase(
   209→            dstPath: "",
   210→            dstSubfolderSpec: .frameworks,
   211→            name: "Embed Frameworks"
   212→        )
   213→
   214→        var frameworkReferences: [GraphDependencyReference] = []
   215→        let productRefs = pbxproj.buildFiles.compactMap(\.product)
   216→
   217→        for dependency in embeddableFrameworks {
   218→            switch dependency {
   219→            case .framework:
   220→                frameworkReferences.append(dependency)
   221→            case let .xcframework(path, _, _, _, condition):
   222→                guard let fileRef = fileElements.file(path: path) else {
   223→                    throw LinkGeneratorError.missingReference(path: path)
   224→                }
   225→                let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(
   226→                    file: fileRef,
   227→                    settings: ["ATTRIBUTES": ["CodeSignOnCopy", "RemoveHeadersOnCopy"]]
   228→                )
   229→                buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   230→                pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   231→                embedPhase.files?.append(buildFile)
   232→            case let .product(dependencyTarget, _, _, condition):
   233→                guard let fileRef = fileElements.product(target: dependencyTarget) else {
   234→                    throw LinkGeneratorError.missingProduct(name: dependencyTarget)
   235→                }
   236→                let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(
   237→                    file: fileRef,
   238→                    settings: ["ATTRIBUTES": ["CodeSignOnCopy", "RemoveHeadersOnCopy"]]
   239→                )
   240→                buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   241→                pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   242→                embedPhase.files?.append(buildFile)
   243→            case let .packageProduct(product, condition):
   244→                guard let productRef = productRefs.first(where: { $0.productName == product }) else {
   245→                    break
   246→                }
   247→
   248→                let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(
   249→                    product: productRef,
   250→                    settings: ["ATTRIBUTES": ["CodeSignOnCopy", "RemoveHeadersOnCopy"]]
   251→                )
   252→                buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   253→                pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   254→                embedPhase.files?.append(buildFile)
   255→            case let .foreignBuildOutput(path, linking, condition):
   256→                if linking == .dynamic {
   257→                    guard let fileRef = fileElements.file(path: path) else {
   258→                        throw LinkGeneratorError.missingReference(path: path)
   259→                    }
   260→                    let buildFile = PBXBuildFile(
   261→                        file: fileRef,
   262→                        settings: ["ATTRIBUTES": ["CodeSignOnCopy", "RemoveHeadersOnCopy"]]
   263→                    )
   264→                    buildFile.applyCondition(condition, applicableTo: target)
   265→                    pbxproj.add(object: buildFile)
   266→                    embedPhase.files?.append(buildFile)
   267→                }
   268→            case .library, .bundle, .sdk, .macro:
   269→                // Do nothing
   270→                break
   271→            }
   272→        }
   273→
   274→        if !frameworkReferences.isEmpty {
   275→            let precompiledEmbedPhase = PBXShellScriptBuildPhase(name: "Embed Precompiled Frameworks")
   276→
   277→            let script = try embedScriptGenerator.script(
   278→                sourceRootPath: sourceRootPath,
   279→                frameworkReferences: frameworkReferences,
   280→                includeSymbolsInFileLists: !target.product.testsBundle
   281→            )
   282→
   283→            precompiledEmbedPhase.shellScript = script.script
   284→            precompiledEmbedPhase.inputPaths = script.inputPaths
   285→                .map { "$(SRCROOT)/\($0.pathString)" }
   286→            precompiledEmbedPhase.outputPaths = script.outputPaths
   287→
   288→            pbxproj.add(object: precompiledEmbedPhase)
   289→            pbxTarget.buildPhases.append(precompiledEmbedPhase)
   290→        }
   291→
   292→        pbxproj.add(object: embedPhase)
   293→        pbxTarget.buildPhases.append(embedPhase)
   294→    }

   238→    func convertToGraphTargetReferences(
   239→        _ dependencies: [(name: String, path: Path.AbsolutePath)],
   240→        for target: GraphDependency
   241→    ) -> [GraphTargetReference] {
   242→        dependencies.compactMap { dependencyName, dependencyPath -> GraphTargetReference? in
   243→            guard let projectDependencies = graph.projects[dependencyPath]?.targets,
   244→                  let dependencyTarget = projectDependencies[dependencyName],
   245→                  let dependencyProject = graph.projects[dependencyPath]
   246→            else {
   247→                return nil
   248→            }
   249→            let condition = graph.dependencyConditions[
   250→                (target, .target(name: dependencyTarget.name, path: dependencyPath))
   251→            ]
   252→            let graphTarget = GraphTarget(
   253→                path: dependencyPath, target: dependencyTarget, project: dependencyProject
   254→            )
   255→            return GraphTargetReference(target: graphTarget, condition: condition)
   256→        }
   257→    }
